topic: LLM API Pricing Comparison 2026: GPT-5 vs Claude vs Gemini vs DeepSeek Costs

key_points: Covers 306+ LLM APIs from OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, DeepSeek, Mistral, xAI
Live pricing updated daily (last updated April 23, 2026)
Highlights best value models per tier
Pricing varies 600x across major LLM APIs ($0.05 to $30 per million input tokens)
Task/volume/quality tradeoff framework for choosing models
DeepSeek V3.2 at $0.14/M input, Gemini 2.5 Flash-Lite at $0.10/M
GPT-5.4 at $2.50/M input, Claude Sonnet at $3/$15
GPT-4o Mini ($0.75/M total) best value for most use cases
Gemini 2.5 Flash-Lite ($0.50/M total) cheapest active model
Real cost breakdown: cheapest at $0.02/M (Mistral Nemo) to $375/M (o1-pro) — 18,000x gap
Detailed GPT-5 vs Claude vs Gemini vs DeepSeek comparison

stats.fact: LLM API prices dropped approximately 80% across the board from 2024 to 2026
DeepSeek V3.2 output tokens cost ~$0.28/M vs GPT-5's $15-30/M — over 50-100x cheaper
Price range spans 18,000x from $0.02/M (Mistral Nemo) to $375/M blended (o1-pro)
GPT-5.4: $2.50/M input, $15/M output; GPT-5.5 (released April 23, 2026): $5/M input, $30/M output
Claude Opus 4.6: $5/M input, $25/M output; Claude Sonnet 4.6: $3/M input, $15/M output; Claude Haiku: $0.25/M input, $1.25/M output
Gemini 2.5 Pro: $1.25/M tokens; Gemini 2.5 Flash: $0.30/M input; Gemini 2.0 Flash-Lite: $0.075/$0.30
DeepSeek V3.2: $0.14/M input, $0.28/M output — cheapest major frontier model
Anthropic dropped Opus pricing by 67% and expanded context window to 1M tokens
Google offers up to 1,000 daily free requests on Gemini — most generous free tier
Batch API cuts costs: GPT-5.4 Batch $1.50/$6.00/M, Claude Opus 4.6 Batch $2.00/$10.00/M, Gemini 2.5 Pro Batch $0.75/$3.50/M
GPT-5.5 scores 60 on Artificial Analysis Intelligence Index vs Claude Opus 4.7 (57) and Gemini 3.1 Pro Preview (57)
Claude Opus 4.7 leads on SWE-bench Verified (87.6%) but costs 2x GPT-5.4
Gemini 3.1 Pro is ~5x cheaper than Claude Opus 4.6 and ~4x cheaper than GPT-5.4 for most tasks
Mistral Nemo: $0.02/M tokens for both input and output — cheapest named model available in 2026

angles: DeepSeek disrupted the pricing floor: Chinese open-weight models force Western giants to cut prices or justify premiums
The 18,000x price gap: Choosing between Mistral Nemo ($0.02/M) and o1-pro ($375/M blended) depends entirely on task requirements
Batch API as a cost strategy: How to cut production LLM costs 40-60% with async batch processing
Free tier showdown: Google's 1,000 daily free requests vs OpenAI's limited trial vs Anthropic's metered credits
The hidden cost of context windows: longer contexts = more tokens = higher costs even with cheaper per-token pricing
Best LLM API by use case: chatbots, coding assistants, RAG pipelines, agentic workflows each have different cost-optimal choices
Price-performance inflection points: when it makes sense to pay 10x more for a premium model
Economy tier ($0.15-$0.50/M) is where most high-volume agentic traffic now sits in 2026
keywords: gpt-5 vs claude vs gemini api cost
openai anthropic google api pricing comparison
